body:
Fixes #13

## Summary

- Keep the signed and notarized macOS release path when Apple signing secrets are configured.
- Build ad-hoc signed macOS DMG and ZIP artifacts when Apple signing/notarization secrets are missing, using `scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.cjs` and `@electron/osx-sign` with identity `-`.
- Require stable macOS arm64/x64 DMG and ZIP assets before publishing a release, and smoke-test the mounted app bundle, executable, and signature mode.
- Add macOS DMGs to the Pages e2e release fixture so missing macOS assets fail locally instead of surfacing as `Not available in latest release`.
- Replace release asset `HEAD` checks with ranged `GET` checks and stronger retries to avoid false 404s immediately after upload.

## Reproduction

Release `v0.9.6` was published without macOS release assets because the macOS build job skipped artifacts when Apple secrets were not present. The GitHub Pages download UI then showed macOS as unavailable.

Before screenshot from issue #13:

![macOS unavailable](https://github.com/konard/vk-bot-desktop/blob/issue-13-c24281c684e3/docs/case-studies/issue-13/artifacts/macos-download-unavailable.png?raw=true)

Investigation notes, downloaded run metadata, and CI logs are captured in `docs/case-studies/issue-13/`.

## Verification

- `node --test --test-timeout=30000 tests/desktop-release-workflow.test.js tests/pages-site.test.js`
- `npm test`
- `npm run build:site`
- `npm run test:pages:e2e -- --site-dir site/dist`
- `npm run check` (passes; existing warning remains in `src/bot/runner.js`)

## Notes

The no-Apple-account fallback is ad-hoc signed and not notarized, so macOS users may still see Gatekeeper warnings. It makes the downloads available for direct testing without requiring an Apple Developer account.
title: Fix macOS release availability without Apple signing secrets
